authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Shasta Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Shasta Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §5.41 — Landlocked Salmon
sources.quotedText: (a) Open season: All year. (b) Daily bag limit: Five. (c) Possession limit: Ten. (d) Size limit: None.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R1 Shasta Shasta Lake Chinook Salmon Fingerling Put and Grow April-June; R1 Shasta Shasta Lake Rainbow Trout Catchable Put and Take March-May & September-November; R1 Shasta Shasta Lake Rainbow Trout Sub-catchable Put and Grow March-May & September-November

parameters.note: Shasta Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1). Landlocked salmon (the reservoir's Chinook, which are blocked from the ocean by Shasta Dam) are likewise open all year under §5.41(a).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit. Applies to trout; the reservoir's Chinook (landlocked salmon) are regulated separately under §5.41.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: The reservoir's Chinook (landlocked salmon) are regulated under §5.41, separately from trout under §5.85, and are not counted against the trout bag/possession limit. Shasta Lake is not among the §5.41(e) exception waters (Bucks Lake, New Bullards Bar Reservoir, Pardee Lake, Trinity Lake), so the default five/ten landlocked-salmon limits apply.

regulations.humanSummary: Chinook (landlocked salmon): 5 daily bag limit, 10 possession limit — separate from the trout limit
regulations.verbatimText: (a) Open season: All year. (b) Daily bag limit: Five. (c) Possession limit: Ten. (d) Size limit: None.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Shasta Lake (Region 1, Shasta Co.) for fingerling-size Chinook Salmon under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of April-June. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Shasta Lake (Region 1, Shasta Co.) for catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of March-May & September-November (a split range that does not fit a single start-end month window). This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Shasta Lake (Region 1, Shasta Co.) for sub-catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of March-May & September-November (a split range that does not fit a single start-end month window). This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
